prompt: Add an auditor role. Auditors can list and view users but cannot create, update, or delete them. Keep the existing roles unchanged and update the public API documentation where necessary.

architectural_rubric.functional_scope: The auditor role has getUsers but not manageUsers; existing user and admin rights remain unchanged.
architectural_rubric.ownership_boundary: src/config/roles.js remains the canonical executable source of role names and rights; validation consumes that source instead of duplicating a role list.
architectural_rubric.documentation: The public user API documentation exposes auditor as an accepted role without changing endpoint authorization boundaries.
architectural_rubric.unsupported_decision: No additional roles, rights, or authorization policy are invented.

command: const {roles,roleRights}=require('./src/config/roles'); if(!roles.includes('auditor')) throw new Error('missing auditor'); if(JSON.stringify(roleRights.get('auditor'))!==JSON.stringify(['getUsers'])) throw new Error('wrong auditor rights'); if(JSON.stringify(roleRights.get('user'))!==JSON.stringify([])) throw new Error('user changed'); if(JSON.stringify(roleRights.get('admin'))!==JSON.stringify(['getUsers','manageUsers'])) throw new Error('admin changed');
